contract:
1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 "Controller" means the entity that determines the purposes and means of the Processing of Personal Data.  
1.2 "Processor" means the entity that Processes Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.  
1.3 "Personal Data" means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person Processed by Processor on behalf of Controller under the Agreement.  
1.4 "Processing" or "Process" means any operation or set of operations performed on Personal Data, whether or not by automated means, including collection, recording, organization, structuring, storage, adaptation, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure, dissemination, alignment, combination, restriction, erasure, or destruction.  
1.5 "Sub-Processor" means any third party engaged by Processor to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
1.6 "Data Protection Laws" means all applicable laws and regulations relating to the Processing of Personal Data, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR").  
1.7 "Data Breach" means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored, or otherwise Processed.  
1.8 "Agreement" means the main agreement between Controller and Processor to which this Data Processing Addendum ("DPA") is attached.

2. ROLES AND SCOPE OF PROCESSING  
2.1 The parties acknowledge that, for the purposes of Data Protection Laws, Controller is the Controller and Processor is the Processor with respect to Personal Data.  
2.2 Processor shall Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a third country, unless required to do so by law.  
2.3 The subject matter, duration, nature, and purpose of the Processing, the types of Personal Data, and categories of Data Subjects are set out in Annex 1 to this DPA.

3. SUB-PROCESSORS  
3.1 Processor may engage Sub-Processors to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller, provided that:  
(a) Processor maintains an up-to-date list of Sub-Processors and provides Controller with at least thirty (30) days’ prior written notice of any intended addition or replacement of Sub-Processors;  
(b) Controller may object to such changes within ten (10) days of receipt of notice;  
(c) Processor imposes data protection obligations on Sub-Processors that are no less protective than those set out in this DPA; and  
(d) Processor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of its Sub-Processors.

4. SECURITY MEASURES  
4.1 Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including, as appropriate:  
(a) pseudonymization and encryption of Personal Data;  
(b) the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of Processing systems;  
(c) the ability to restore availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and  
(d) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures.

5. DATA BREACH NOTIFICATION  
5.1 Processor shall notify Controller without undue delay and in any event within forty-eight (48) hours after becoming aware of a Data Breach affecting Personal Data.  
5.2 Such notification shall include, to the extent possible, a description of the nature of the Data Breach, the categories and approximate number of Data Subjects concerned, the likely consequences, and the measures taken or proposed to address the Data Breach.

6. AUDIT RIGHTS  
6.1 Processor shall make available to Controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA and Data Protection Laws.  
6.2 Controller may, upon at least fifteen (15) business days’ prior written notice and no more than once per calendar year, conduct an audit (including inspections) of Processor’s Processing of Personal Data, provided that such audit shall not unreasonably disrupt Processor’s business and shall be subject to reasonable confidentiality obligations.  
6.3 If the audit reveals material non-compliance, Processor shall promptly take corrective action.

7. DELETION OR RETURN OF PERSONAL DATA  
7.1 Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, Processor shall, at Controller’s choice, delete or return all Personal Data to Controller and delete existing copies, unless applicable law requires storage of the Personal Data.  
7.2 Processor shall certify in writing to Controller that such deletion has been completed within thirty (30) days of termination or expiration.

8. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS  
8.1 Processor shall not transfer Personal Data outside the European Economic Area ("EEA") or the United Kingdom unless it has taken such measures as are necessary to ensure the transfer is in compliance with Data Protection Laws, including, where appropriate, entering into Standard Contractual Clauses or implementing other appropriate safeguards.

9. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
9.1 Sections 4 (Security Measures), 5 (Data Breach Notification), 6 (Audit Rights), and 7 (Deletion or Return of Personal Data) shall survive termination or expiration of this DPA for as long as Processor Processes Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
9.2 Any limitation of liability agreed in the Agreement shall apply to this DPA, provided that Processor’s aggregate liability for Data Breaches shall not exceed EUR 1,000,000.  
9.3 This DPA is governed by the law governing the Agreement.  
9.4 In the event of any conflict between this DPA and the Agreement, this DPA shall prevail with respect to the subject matter herein.  
9.5 All notices under this DPA shall be in writing and delivered to the contacts set forth in the Agreement.

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 "Controller" means the entity that determines the purposes and means of the Processing of Personal Data.  
1.2 "Processor" means the entity that Processes Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.  
1.3 "Personal Data" means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person Processed by Processor on behalf of Controller under the Agreement.  
1.4 "Processing" or "Process" means any operation or set of operations performed on Personal Data, whether or not by automated means, including collection, recording, organization, structuring, storage, adaptation, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure, dissemination, alignment, combination, restriction, erasure, or destruction.  
1.5 "Sub-Processor" means any third party engaged by Processor to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
1.6 "Data Protection Laws" means all applicable laws and regulations relating to the Processing of Personal Data, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR").  
1.7 "Data Breach" means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored, or otherwise Processed.  
1.8 "Agreement" means the main agreement between Controller and Processor to which this Data Processing Addendum ("DPA") is attached.

2. ROLES AND SCOPE OF PROCESSING  
2.1 The parties acknowledge that, for the purposes of Data Protection Laws, Controller is the Controller and Processor is the Processor with respect to Personal Data.  
2.2 Processor shall Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a third country, unless required to do so by law.  
2.3 The subject matter, duration, nature, and purpose of the Processing, the types of Personal Data, and categories of Data Subjects are set out in Annex 1 to this DPA.

3. SUB-PROCESSORS  
3.1 Processor may engage Sub-Processors to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller, provided that:  
(a) Processor maintains an up-to-date list of Sub-Processors and provides Controller with at least thirty (30) days’ prior written notice of any intended addition or replacement of Sub-Processors;  
(b) Controller may object to such changes within ten (10) days of receipt of notice;  
(c) Processor imposes data protection obligations on Sub-Processors that are no less protective than those set out in this DPA; and  
(d) Processor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of its Sub-Processors.

4. SECURITY MEASURES  
4.1 Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including, as appropriate:  
(a) pseudonymization and encryption of Personal Data;  
(b) the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of Processing systems;  
(c) the ability to restore availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and  
(d) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures.

5. DATA BREACH NOTIFICATION  
5.1 Processor shall notify Controller without undue delay and in any event within forty-eight (48) hours after becoming aware of a Data Breach affecting Personal Data.  
5.2 Such notification shall include, to the extent possible, a description of the nature of the Data Breach, the categories and approximate number of Data Subjects concerned, the likely consequences, and the measures taken or proposed to address the Data Breach.

6. AUDIT RIGHTS  
6.1 Processor shall make available to Controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA and Data Protection Laws.  
6.2 Controller may, upon at least fifteen (15) business days’ prior written notice and no more than once per calendar year, conduct an audit (including inspections) of Processor’s Processing of Personal Data, provided that such audit shall not unreasonably disrupt Processor’s business and shall be subject to reasonable confidentiality obligations.  
6.3 If the audit reveals material non-compliance, Processor shall promptly take corrective action.

7. DELETION OR RETURN OF PERSONAL DATA  
7.1 Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, Processor shall, at Controller’s choice, delete or return all Personal Data to Controller and delete existing copies, unless applicable law requires storage of the Personal Data.  
7.2 Processor shall certify in writing to Controller that such deletion has been completed within thirty (30) days of termination or expiration.

8. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS  
8.1 Processor shall not transfer Personal Data outside the European Economic Area ("EEA") or the United Kingdom unless it has taken such measures as are necessary to ensure the transfer is in compliance with Data Protection Laws, including, where appropriate, entering into Standard Contractual Clauses or implementing other appropriate safeguards.

9. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
9.1 Sections 4 (Security Measures), 5 (Data Breach Notification), 6 (Audit Rights), and 7 (Deletion or Return of Personal Data) shall survive termination or expiration of this DPA for as long as Processor Processes Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
9.2 Any limitation of liability agreed in the Agreement shall apply to this DPA, provided that Processor’s aggregate liability for Data Breaches shall not exceed EUR 1,000,000.  
9.3 This DPA is governed by the law governing the Agreement.  
9.4 In the event of any conflict between this DPA and the Agreement, this DPA shall prevail with respect to the subject matter herein.  
9.5 All notices under this DPA shall be in writing and delivered to the contacts set forth in the Agreement.

1.5 "Sub-Processor" means any third party engaged by Processor to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
1.6 "Data Protection Laws" means all applicable laws and regulations relating to the Processing of Personal Data, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR").  
1.7 "Data Breach" means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored, or otherwise Processed.  
1.8 "Agreement" means the main agreement between Controller and Processor to which this Data Processing Addendum ("DPA") is attached.

2. ROLES AND SCOPE OF PROCESSING  
2.1 The parties acknowledge that, for the purposes of Data Protection Laws, Controller is the Controller and Processor is the Processor with respect to Personal Data.  
2.2 Processor shall Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a third country, unless required to do so by law.  
2.3 The subject matter, duration, nature, and purpose of the Processing, the types of Personal Data, and categories of Data Subjects are set out in Annex 1 to this DPA.

3. SUB-PROCESSORS  
3.1 Processor may engage Sub-Processors to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller, provided that:  
(a) Processor maintains an up-to-date list of Sub-Processors and provides Controller with at least thirty (30) days’ prior written notice of any intended addition or replacement of Sub-Processors;  
(b) Controller may object to such changes within ten (10) days of receipt of notice;  
(c) Processor imposes data protection obligations on Sub-Processors that are no less protective than those set out in this DPA; and  
(d) Processor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of its Sub-Processors.

4. SECURITY MEASURES  
4.1 Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including, as appropriate:  
(a) pseudonymization and encryption of Personal Data;  
(b) the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of Processing systems;  
(c) the ability to restore availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and  
(d) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures.

5. DATA BREACH NOTIFICATION  
5.1 Processor shall notify Controller without undue delay and in any event within forty-eight (48) hours after becoming aware of a Data Breach affecting Personal Data.

(a) Processor maintains an up-to-date list of Sub-Processors and provides Controller with at least thirty (30) days’ prior written notice of any intended addition or replacement of Sub-Processors;

(c) Processor imposes data protection obligations on Sub-Processors that are no less protective than those set out in this DPA; and

(c) the ability to restore availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and  
(d) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures.

1.6 "Data Protection Laws" means all applicable laws and regulations relating to the Processing of Personal Data, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR").  
1.7 "Data Breach" means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored, or otherwise Processed.  
1.8 "Agreement" means the main agreement between Controller and Processor to which this Data Processing Addendum ("DPA") is attached.

2. ROLES AND SCOPE OF PROCESSING  
2.1 The parties acknowledge that, for the purposes of Data Protection Laws, Controller is the Controller and Processor is the Processor with respect to Personal Data.  
2.2 Processor shall Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a third country, unless required to do so by law.  
2.3 The subject matter, duration, nature, and purpose of the Processing, the types of Personal Data, and categories of Data Subjects are set out in Annex 1 to this DPA.

3. SUB-PROCESSORS  
3.1 Processor may engage Sub-Processors to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller, provided that:  
(a) Processor maintains an up-to-date list of Sub-Processors and provides Controller with at least thirty (30) days’ prior written notice of any intended addition or replacement of Sub-Processors;  
(b) Controller may object to such changes within ten (10) days of receipt of notice;  
(c) Processor imposes data protection obligations on Sub-Processors that are no less protective than those set out in this DPA; and  
(d) Processor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of its Sub-Processors.

4. SECURITY MEASURES  
4.1 Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including, as appropriate:  
(a) pseudonymization and encryption of Personal Data;  
(b) the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of Processing systems;  
(c) the ability to restore availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and  
(d) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures.

5. DATA BREACH NOTIFICATION

7.1 Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, Processor shall, at Controller’s choice, delete or return all Personal Data to Controller and delete existing copies, unless applicable law requires storage of the Personal Data.

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 "Controller" means the entity that determines the purposes and means of the Processing of Personal Data.  
1.2 "Processor" means the entity that Processes Personal Data on behalf of the Controller.  
1.3 "Personal Data" means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person Processed by Processor on behalf of Controller under the Agreement.  
1.4 "Processing" or "Process" means any operation or set of operations performed on Personal Data, whether or not by automated means, including collection, recording, organization, structuring, storage, adaptation, retrieval, consultation, use, disclosure, dissemination, alignment, combination, restriction, erasure, or destruction.  
1.5 "Sub-Processor" means any third party engaged by Processor to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
1.6 "Data Protection Laws" means all applicable laws and regulations relating to the Processing of Personal Data, including the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 ("GDPR").  
1.7 "Data Breach" means a breach of security leading to the accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorized disclosure of, or access to, Personal Data transmitted, stored, or otherwise Processed.  
1.8 "Agreement" means the main agreement between Controller and Processor to which this Data Processing Addendum ("DPA") is attached.

2. ROLES AND SCOPE OF PROCESSING  
2.1 The parties acknowledge that, for the purposes of Data Protection Laws, Controller is the Controller and Processor is the Processor with respect to Personal Data.  
2.2 Processor shall Process Personal Data only on documented instructions from Controller, including with regard to transfers of Personal Data to a third country, unless required to do so by law.  
2.3 The subject matter, duration, nature, and purpose of the Processing, the types of Personal Data, and categories of Data Subjects are set out in Annex 1 to this DPA.

3. SUB-PROCESSORS  
3.1 Processor may engage Sub-Processors to Process Personal Data on behalf of Controller, provided that:  
(a) Processor maintains an up-to-date list of Sub-Processors and provides Controller with at least thirty (30) days’ prior written notice of any intended addition or replacement of Sub-Processors;  
(b) Controller may object to such changes within ten (10) days of receipt of notice;  
(c) Processor imposes data protection obligations on Sub-Processors that are no less protective than those set out in this DPA; and  
(d) Processor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of its Sub-Processors.

4. SECURITY MEASURES  
4.1 Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including, as appropriate:  
(a) pseudonymization and encryption of Personal Data;  
(b) the ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of Processing systems;  
(c) the ability to restore availability and access to Personal Data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; and  
(d) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures.

5. DATA BREACH NOTIFICATION  
5.1 Processor shall notify Controller without undue delay and in any event within forty-eight (48) hours after becoming aware of a Data Breach affecting Personal Data.  
5.2 Such notification shall include, to the extent possible, a description of the nature of the Data Breach, the categories and approximate number of Data Subjects concerned, the likely consequences, and the measures taken or proposed to address the Data Breach.

6. AUDIT RIGHTS  
6.1 Processor shall make available to Controller all information necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA and Data Protection Laws.  
6.2 Controller may, upon at least fifteen (15) business days’ prior written notice and no more than once per calendar year, conduct an audit (including inspections) of Processor’s Processing of Personal Data, provided that such audit shall not unreasonably disrupt Processor’s business and shall be subject to reasonable confidentiality obligations.  
6.3 If the audit reveals material non-compliance, Processor shall promptly take corrective action.

7. DELETION OR RETURN OF PERSONAL DATA  
7.1 Upon termination or expiration of the Agreement, Processor shall, at Controller’s choice, delete or return all Personal Data to Controller and delete existing copies, unless applicable law requires storage of the Personal Data.  
7.2 Processor shall certify in writing to Controller that such deletion has been completed within thirty (30) days of termination or expiration.

8. INTERNATIONAL DATA TRANSFERS  
8.1 Processor shall not transfer Personal Data outside the European Economic Area ("EEA") or the United Kingdom unless it has taken such measures as are necessary to ensure the transfer is in compliance with Data Protection Laws, including, where appropriate, entering into Standard Contractual Clauses or implementing other appropriate safeguards.

9. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
9.1 Sections 4 (Security Measures), 5 (Data Breach Notification), 6 (Audit Rights), and 7 (Deletion or Return of Personal Data) shall survive termination or expiration of this DPA for as long as Processor Processes Personal Data on behalf of Controller.  
9.2 Any limitation of liability agreed in the Agreement shall apply to this DPA, provided that Processor’s aggregate liability for Data Breaches shall not exceed EUR 1,000,000.  
9.3 This DPA is governed by the law governing the Agreement.  
9.4 In the event of any conflict between this DPA and the Agreement, this DPA shall prevail with respect to the subject matter herein.  
9.5 All notices under this DPA shall be in writing and delivered to the contacts set forth in the Agreement.
claim: Processor shall not transfer Personal Data outside the EEA or UK unless measures necessary to ensure compliance with Data Protection Laws are taken, such as Standard Contractual Clauses or other safeguards.

contract:
MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the Client and the Service Provider, as identified in the applicable Statement of Work (“SOW”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Affiliate” means any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party.  
1.2 “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by either party to the other, whether oral, written, or electronic, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential.  
1.3 “Deliverables” means all work product, reports, materials, and other items to be delivered by Service Provider to Client under any SOW.  
1.4 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature on the first SOW referencing this Agreement.  
1.5 “Fees” means the amounts payable by Client to Service Provider for the Services, as set forth in the applicable SOW.  
1.6 “Intellectual Property Rights” means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.  
1.7 “Personal Data” means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person processed by Service Provider on behalf of Client.  
1.8 “Services” means the professional services to be provided by Service Provider to Client as described in any SOW.  
1.9 “SLA” means the service level agreement, if any, attached to or referenced in an SOW.  
1.10 “Term” means the duration of this Agreement as set forth in Section 8.

2. SERVICES  
2.1 Service Provider shall perform the Services and deliver the Deliverables in accordance with the applicable SOW and any SLA.  
2.2 Service Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to meet or exceed any SLA targets, including a minimum uptime of 99.5% per calendar month for any hosted services.

3. PAYMENT TERMS  
3.1 Client shall pay all Fees within thirty (30) days of receipt of a valid invoice.  
3.2 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.  
3.3 Service Provider may suspend Services upon ten (10) days’ written notice if undisputed Fees remain unpaid after the due date.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  
4.1 Except as otherwise set forth in an SOW, all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables (excluding Service Provider’s pre-existing materials and third-party materials) shall be assigned to Client upon full payment of Fees.  
4.2 Service Provider retains all rights in its pre-existing materials and may use general knowledge, skills, and experience acquired during performance of the Services.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY  
5.1 Each party shall protect the other’s Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care.  
5.2 Confidential Information may only be used for purposes of performing obligations under this Agreement and may be disclosed only to employees, Affiliates, or contractors with a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations.  
5.3 The obligations in this Section 5 shall survive for three (3) years after termination of this Agreement, except for trade secrets, which shall be protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

6. DATA PROTECTION  
6.1 Service Provider shall process Personal Data only as necessary to perform the Services and in accordance with applicable data protection laws.  
6.2 Service Provider shall implement reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect Personal Data against unauthorized access, loss, or disclosure.  
6.3 If Service Provider becomes aware of a data breach affecting Personal Data, it shall notify Client without undue delay and cooperate with Client’s reasonable requests regarding remediation.

7. WARRANTIES  
7.1 Service Provider warrants that the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner and that Deliverables will materially conform to the specifications in the applicable SOW for ninety (90) days after delivery.  
7.2 EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT, SERVICE PROVIDER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

8. TERM AND TERMINATION  
8.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated by either party upon sixty (60) days’ written notice, or as otherwise set forth in an SOW.  
8.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for material breach by the other party if such breach is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice.  
8.3 Upon termination, Client shall pay Service Provider for all Services performed and Deliverables delivered up to the effective date of termination.

9. INDEMNITY  
9.1 Service Provider shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client from and against any third-party claims alleging that the Deliverables infringe any Intellectual Property Rights, provided Client promptly notifies Service Provider and allows Service Provider to control the defense.  
9.2 Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Service Provider from and against any third-party claims arising from Client’s misuse of the Services or Deliverables.

10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY  
10.1 EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY, OR INDEMNITY OBLIGATIONS, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES.  
10.2 EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO SERVICE PROVIDER UNDER THE APPLICABLE SOW IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR $250,000, WHICHEVER IS LESS.

11. GOVERNING LAW  
11.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.

12. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
12.1 Sections 4 (Intellectual Property), 5 (Confidentiality), 6 (Data Protection), 9 (Indemnity), 10 (Limitation of Liability), and 12 (Survival and Miscellaneous) shall survive termination of this Agreement.  
12.2 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses specified in the SOW.  
12.3 This Agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
12.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
12.5 Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except to an Affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.  
12.6 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
claim: Service Provider must perform Services and deliver Deliverables as specified in the applicable SOW and any referenced SLA.

MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the Client and the Service Provider, as identified in the applicable Statement of Work (“SOW”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Affiliate” means any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party.  
1.2 “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by either party to the other, whether oral, written, or electronic, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential.  
1.3 “Deliverables” means all work product, reports, materials, and other items to be delivered by Service Provider to Client under any SOW.  
1.4 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature on the first SOW referencing this Agreement.  
1.5 “Fees” means the amounts payable by Client to Service Provider for the Services, as set forth in the applicable SOW.  
1.6 “Intellectual Property Rights” means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.  
1.7 “Personal Data” means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person processed by Service Provider on behalf of Client.  
1.8 “Services” means the professional services to be provided by Service Provider to Client as described in any SOW.  
1.9 “SLA” means the service level agreement, if any, attached to or referenced in an SOW.  
1.10 “Term” means the duration of this Agreement as set forth in Section 8.

2. SERVICES  
2.1 Service Provider shall perform the Services and deliver the Deliverables in accordance with the applicable SOW and any SLA.  
2.2 Service Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to meet or exceed any SLA targets, including a minimum uptime of 99.5% per calendar month for any hosted services.

3. PAYMENT TERMS  
3.1 Client shall pay all Fees within thirty (30) days of receipt of a valid invoice.  
3.2 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.  
3.3 Service Provider may suspend Services upon ten (10) days’ written notice if undisputed Fees remain unpaid after the due date.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  
4.1 Except as otherwise set forth in an SOW, all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables (excluding Service Provider’s pre-existing materials and third-party materials) shall be assigned to Client upon full payment of Fees.  
4.2 Service Provider retains all rights in its pre-existing materials and may use general knowledge, skills, and experience acquired during performance of the Services.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY  
5.1 Each party shall protect the other’s Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care.  
5.2 Confidential Information may only be used for purposes of performing obligations under this Agreement and may be disclosed only to employees, Affiliates, or contractors with a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations.  
5.3 The obligations in this Section 5 shall survive for three (3) years after termination of this Agreement, except for trade secrets, which shall be protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

6. DATA PROTECTION  
6.1 Service Provider shall process Personal Data only as necessary to perform the Services and in accordance with applicable data protection laws.  
6.2 Service Provider shall implement reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect Personal Data against unauthorized access, loss, or disclosure.  
6.3 If Service Provider becomes aware of a data breach affecting Personal Data, it shall notify Client without undue delay and cooperate with Client’s reasonable requests regarding remediation.

7. WARRANTIES  
7.1 Service Provider warrants that the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner and that Deliverables will materially conform to the specifications in the applicable SOW for ninety (90) days after delivery.  
7.2 EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT, SERVICE PROVIDER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

8. TERM AND TERMINATION  
8.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated by either party upon sixty (60) days’ written notice, or as otherwise set forth in an SOW.  
8.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for material breach by the other party if such breach is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice.  
8.3 Upon termination, Client shall pay Service Provider for all Services performed and Deliverables delivered up to the effective date of termination.

9. INDEMNITY  
9.1 Service Provider shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client from and against any third-party claims alleging that the Deliverables infringe any Intellectual Property Rights, provided Client promptly notifies Service Provider and allows Service Provider to control the defense.  
9.2 Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Service Provider from and against any third-party claims arising from Client’s misuse of the Services or Deliverables.

10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY  
10.1 EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY, OR INDEMNITY OBLIGATIONS, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES.  
10.2 EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO SERVICE PROVIDER UNDER THE APPLICABLE SOW IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR $250,000, WHICHEVER IS LESS.

11. GOVERNING LAW  
11.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.

12. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
12.1 Sections 4 (Intellectual Property), 5 (Confidentiality), 6 (Data Protection), 9 (Indemnity), 10 (Limitation of Liability), and 12 (Survival and Miscellaneous) shall survive termination of this Agreement.  
12.2 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses specified in the SOW.  
12.3 This Agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
12.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
12.5 Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except to an Affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.  
12.6 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

1.4 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature on the first SOW referencing this Agreement.  
1.5 “Fees” means the amounts payable by Client to Service Provider for the Services, as set forth in the applicable SOW.  
1.6 “Intellectual Property Rights” means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.  
1.7 “Personal Data” means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person processed by Service Provider on behalf of Client.  
1.8 “Services” means the professional services to be provided by Service Provider to Client as described in any SOW.  
1.9 “SLA” means the service level agreement, if any, attached to or referenced in an SOW.

2. SERVICES  
2.1 Service Provider shall perform the Services and deliver the Deliverables in accordance with the applicable SOW and any SLA.  
2.2 Service Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to meet or exceed any SLA targets, including a minimum uptime of 99.5% per calendar month for any hosted services.

3. PAYMENT TERMS  
3.1 Client shall pay all Fees within thirty (30) days of receipt of a valid invoice.  
3.2 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.  
3.3 Service Provider may suspend Services upon ten (10) days’ written notice if undisputed Fees remain unpaid after the due date.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

5. CONFIDENTIALITY

12. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS

12.2 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses specified in the SOW.  
12.3 This Agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
12.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
12.5 Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except to an Affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.

MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the Client and the Service Provider, as identified in the applicable Statement of Work (“SOW”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Affiliate” means any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party.  
1.2 “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by either party to the other, whether oral, written, or electronic, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential.  
1.3 “Deliverables” means all work product, reports, materials, and other items to be delivered by Service Provider to Client under any SOW.  
1.4 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature on the first SOW referencing this Agreement.  
1.5 “Fees” means the amounts payable by Client to Service Provider for the Services, as set forth in the applicable SOW.  
1.6 “Intellectual Property Rights” means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.  
1.7 “Personal Data” means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person processed by Service Provider on behalf of Client.  
1.8 “Services” means the professional services to be provided by Service Provider to Client as described in any SOW.  
1.9 “SLA” means the service level agreement, if any, attached to or referenced in an SOW.  
1.10 “Term” means the duration of this Agreement as set forth in Section 8.

2. SERVICES  
2.1 Service Provider shall perform the Services and deliver the Deliverables in accordance with the applicable SOW and any SLA.  
2.2 Service Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to meet or exceed any SLA targets, including a minimum uptime of 99.5% per calendar month for any hosted services.

3. PAYMENT TERMS  
3.1 Client shall pay all Fees within thirty (30) days of receipt of a valid invoice.  
3.2 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.  
3.3 Service Provider may suspend Services upon ten (10) days’ written notice if undisputed Fees remain unpaid after the due date.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  
4.1 Except as otherwise set forth in an SOW, all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables (excluding Service Provider’s pre-existing materials and third-party materials) shall be assigned to Client upon full payment of Fees.  
4.2 Service Provider retains all rights in its pre-existing materials and may use general knowledge, skills, and experience acquired during performance of the Services.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY  
5.1 Each party shall protect the other’s Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care.  
5.2 Confidential Information may only be used for purposes of performing obligations under this Agreement and may be disclosed only to employees, Affiliates, or contractors with a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations.  
5.3 The obligations in this Section 5 shall survive for three (3) years after termination of this Agreement, except for trade secrets, which shall be protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

6. DATA PROTECTION  
6.1 Service Provider shall process Personal Data only as necessary to perform the Services and in accordance with applicable data protection laws.  
6.2 Service Provider shall implement reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect Personal Data against unauthorized access, loss, or disclosure.  
6.3 If Service Provider becomes aware of a data breach affecting Personal Data, it shall notify Client without undue delay and cooperate with Client’s reasonable requests regarding remediation.

7. WARRANTIES  
7.1 Service Provider warrants that the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner and that Deliverables will materially conform to the specifications in the applicable SOW for ninety (90) days after delivery.  
7.2 EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT, SERVICE PROVIDER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

8. TERM AND TERMINATION  
8.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated by either party upon sixty (60) days’ written notice, or as otherwise set forth in an SOW.  
8.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for material breach by the other party if such breach is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice.  
8.3 Upon termination, Client shall pay Service Provider for all Services performed and Deliverables delivered up to the effective date of termination.

9. INDEMNITY  
9.1 Service Provider shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client from and against any third-party claims alleging that the Deliverables infringe any Intellectual Property Rights, provided Client promptly notifies Service Provider and allows Service Provider to control the defense.  
9.2 Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Service Provider from and against any third-party claims arising from Client’s misuse of the Services or Deliverables.

10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY  
10.1 EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY, OR INDEMNITY OBLIGATIONS, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES.  
10.2 EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO SERVICE PROVIDER UNDER THE APPLICABLE SOW IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR $250,000, WHICHEVER IS LESS.

11. GOVERNING LAW  
11.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.

12. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
12.1 Sections 4 (Intellectual Property), 5 (Confidentiality), 6 (Data Protection), 9 (Indemnity), 10 (Limitation of Liability), and 12 (Survival and Miscellaneous) shall survive termination of this Agreement.  
12.2 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses specified in the SOW.  
12.3 This Agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
12.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
12.5 Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except to an Affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.  
12.6 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.

MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT

This Master Services Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into as of the Effective Date by and between the Client and the Service Provider, as identified in the applicable Statement of Work (“SOW”).

1. DEFINITIONS  
1.1 “Affiliate” means any entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with a party.  
1.2 “Confidential Information” means all non-public information disclosed by either party to the other, whether oral, written, or electronic, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential.  
1.3 “Deliverables” means all work product, reports, materials, and other items to be delivered by Service Provider to Client under any SOW.  
1.4 “Effective Date” means the date of last signature on the first SOW referencing this Agreement.  
1.5 “Fees” means the amounts payable by Client to Service Provider for the Services, as set forth in the applicable SOW.  
1.6 “Intellectual Property Rights” means all patents, copyrights, trademarks, trade secrets, and other proprietary rights.  
1.7 “Personal Data” means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person processed by Service Provider on behalf of Client.  
1.8 “Services” means the professional services to be provided by Service Provider to Client as described in any SOW.  
1.9 “SLA” means the service level agreement, if any, attached to or referenced in an SOW.  
1.10 “Term” means the duration of this Agreement as set forth in Section 8.

2. SERVICES  
2.1 Service Provider shall perform the Services and deliver the Deliverables in accordance with the applicable SOW and any SLA.  
2.2 Service Provider will use commercially reasonable efforts to meet or exceed any SLA targets, including a minimum uptime of 99.5% per calendar month for any hosted services.

3. PAYMENT TERMS  
3.1 Client shall pay all Fees within thirty (30) days of receipt of a valid invoice.  
3.2 Late payments shall accrue interest at a rate of 1.5% per month or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is less.  
3.3 Service Provider may suspend Services upon ten (10) days’ written notice if undisputed Fees remain unpaid after the due date.

4. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY  
4.1 Except as otherwise set forth in an SOW, all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables (excluding Service Provider’s pre-existing materials and third-party materials) shall be assigned to Client upon full payment of Fees.  
4.2 Service Provider retains all rights in its pre-existing materials and may use general knowledge, skills, and experience acquired during performance of the Services.

5. CONFIDENTIALITY  
5.1 Each party shall protect the other’s Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care.  
5.2 Confidential Information may only be used for purposes of performing obligations under this Agreement and may be disclosed only to employees, Affiliates, or contractors with a need to know and who are bound by confidentiality obligations.  
5.3 The obligations in this Section 5 shall survive for three (3) years after termination of this Agreement, except for trade secrets, which shall be protected as long as they remain trade secrets.

6. DATA PROTECTION  
6.1 Service Provider shall process Personal Data only as necessary to perform the Services and in accordance with applicable data protection laws.  
6.2 Service Provider shall implement reasonable technical and organizational measures to protect Personal Data against unauthorized access, loss, or disclosure.  
6.3 If Service Provider becomes aware of a data breach affecting Personal Data, it shall notify Client without undue delay and cooperate with Client’s reasonable requests regarding remediation.

7. WARRANTIES  
7.1 Service Provider warrants that the Services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner and that Deliverables will materially conform to the specifications in the applicable SOW for ninety (90) days after delivery.  
7.2 EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY PROVIDED IN THIS AGREEMENT, SERVICE PROVIDER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING ANY WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.

8. TERM AND TERMINATION  
8.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues until terminated by either party upon sixty (60) days’ written notice, or as otherwise set forth in an SOW.  
8.2 Either party may terminate this Agreement or any SOW for material breach by the other party if such breach is not cured within thirty (30) days after written notice.  
8.3 Upon termination, Client shall pay Service Provider for all Services performed and Deliverables delivered up to the effective date of termination.

9. INDEMNITY  
9.1 Service Provider shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Client from and against any third-party claims alleging that the Deliverables infringe any Intellectual Property Rights, provided Client promptly notifies Service Provider and allows Service Provider to control the defense.  
9.2 Client shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Service Provider from and against any third-party claims arising from Client’s misuse of the Services or Deliverables.

10. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY  
10.1 EXCEPT FOR LIABILITY ARISING FROM GROSS NEGLIGENCE, WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY, OR INDEMNITY OBLIGATIONS, NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES.  
10.2 EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY UNDER THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO SERVICE PROVIDER UNDER THE APPLICABLE SOW IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM, OR $250,000, WHICHEVER IS LESS.

11. GOVERNING LAW  
11.1 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York, without regard to its conflict of law principles.

12. SURVIVAL AND MISCELLANEOUS  
12.1 Sections 4 (Intellectual Property), 5 (Confidentiality), 6 (Data Protection), 9 (Indemnity), 10 (Limitation of Liability), and 12 (Survival and Miscellaneous) shall survive termination of this Agreement.  
12.2 Notices under this Agreement must be in writing and delivered to the addresses specified in the SOW.  
12.3 This Agreement, together with all SOWs, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior agreements.  
12.4 No amendment or waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless in writing and signed by both parties.  
12.5 Neither party may assign this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, except to an Affiliate or in connection with a merger or sale of substantially all assets.  
12.6 If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
